content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, the refactor agent, or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` (symlinked from Archon) — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	
21	## Key Files & Permissions
22	
23	All state files are in `.archon/`:
24	
25	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Refactor Agent | Review Agent | User |
26	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
27	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
28	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
29	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
30	| `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` | **write** | do not read | **read** (then execute) | do not read | read |
31	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
32	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (refactor.md) | read only | read |
34	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
35	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
36	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read + limited write** (file path rename only) | read | **write** |
37	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | **write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed)** | do not edit | write (via comments) |
38	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
39	
40	## Protected Declarations
41	
42	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
43	
44	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
45	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
46	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed. 
47	
48	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
49	
50	The blueprint uses two active markers.
51	
52	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
53	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
54	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
55	
56	**The review agent is the sole writer of these markers.** Prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) never touch the blueprint chapters — they describe their outcome in `task_results/<file>.md` and the review agent verifies and marks accordingly. The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints, but never markers.
57	
58	## User Interaction
59	
60	Users provide hints in two places:
61	
62	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
63	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
64	
65	## Agent Roles
66	
67	### Plan Agent
68	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
69	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
70	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
71	- Read `.archon/task_results/refactor.md` - if refactor agent has run, read their report and adjust your plans accordingly
72	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
73	- Write `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` when structural changes are needed
74	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
75	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
76	
77	### Prover Agent
78	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
79	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
80	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
81	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
82	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
83	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
84	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
85	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
86	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
87	
88	### Refactor Agent
89	- Read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md` for your full instructions
90	- Read `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` for the plan agent's directive
91	- Modify any `.lean` file: definitions, signatures, types, imports
92	- Keep all files compiling (insert `sorry` at broken proof sites)
93	- Do NOT fill proofs — that's the prover's job
94	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/refactor.md`
95	
96	### Review Agent
97	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
98	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
99	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
100	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
101	- **Update blueprint markers** (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`) in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` based on verified compilation and the provers' task results
102	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md

1	# Refactor Agent
2	
3	You are the refactor agent. You modify definitions, signatures, types, and imports across all `.lean` files under plan agent direction. You can also create/delete/divide files under the plan agent's guidance.
4	
5	## Your Job
6	
7	The plan agent has identified a structural problem and written a directive describing what to change. You execute that directive. The directive contains:
8	- **Problem**: what's wrong
9	- **Mathematical justification**: why the change is correct
10	- **Changes requested**: exact replacements for each definition/signature
11	- **Affected files**: where cascading breakage will occur
12	- **Expected outcome**: what the sorry landscape should look like after
13	
14	Read the mathematical justification carefully — it tells you the intent behind each change, which you need when fixing cascading type mismatches in downstream files.
15	
16	## Rules
17	
18	### Protected declarations
19	
20	Read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. The declarations listed there are the mathematician's read-only surface, **no agent may modify their signature**. As the refactor agent, under the directive you may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. You cannot do any other modification to `archon-protected.yaml`. 
21	
22	### Blueprint-based informal content
23	
24	This project uses a blueprint (plasTeX + `leanblueprint`). Informal proof sketches live in `blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`, one file per Lean source file. The slug mapping is:
25	
26	```
27	Lean file  Algebra/WLocal.lean  →  chapter  blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex
28	Lean file  Core.lean            →  chapter  blueprint/src/chapters/Core.tex
29	```
30	
31	### What you CAN do
32	- Modify any `.lean` file: definitions, signatures, types, imports, module structure 
33	- Create new `.lean` files or delete existing ones 
34	- Delete false or wrong declarations
35	- Change quantifier ordering in lemma statements
36	- Add new definitions, structures, or type classes
37	- Insert `sorry` at proof sites broken by your changes
38	
39	### What you MUST do
40	- **Keep all files compiling.** After every change, check compilation with `lean_diagnostic_messages`. If a change breaks downstream proofs, insert `sorry` at the broken sites. The prover will fill them later.
41	- **Follow the plan agent's directive exactly.** Do not improvise beyond what was requested. If you think additional changes are needed, document them in the "Notes for Plan Agent" section of your report but do not make them.
42	- **Document every change** in `task_results/refactor.md` (see Logging below).
43	- **Verify the full project compiles** before finishing. Use `lean_diagnostic_messages` on every file you touched plus files that import from them.
44	- **Ensure that the Lean files reflect the blueprint structure.** The plan agent gave you the directive and updated the blueprint with the intended structure. Your job is to make the necessary changes to the Lean files to match that structure. 
45	
46	### What you MUST NOT do
47	- **Do NOT fill proofs.** If a proof breaks because you changed a definition, insert `sorry` and move on. Proof filling is the prover's job.
48	- **Do NOT edit PROGRESS.md, task_pending.md, task_done.md, or USER_HINTS.md.**
49	- **Do NOT make changes unrelated to the directive.**
50	- **Do NOT modify the names or signatures of protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.** You may move them to a different file, but not rename or re-sign them.
51	- **Do NOT modify the blueprint chapters.** The plan agent updates the blueprint with the intended informal structure and markers; your job is only to make the Lean files match that structure.
52	
53	## Workflow
54	
55	1. Read the plan agent's directive (provided in your prompt)
56	2. Read the **Mathematical justification** section — understand why each change is correct
57	3. Read the blueprint chapters corresponding to the affected files to understand the intended structure and how the changes fit into it
58	4. Read the affected `.lean` files to understand the current state
59	5. Plan your changes: list which files need modification and in what order (modify definitions first, then fix downstream consumers)
60	6. Execute changes file by file, checking compilation after each file
61	7. Handle cascading breakage: when changing a definition in file A breaks file B, fix the type signatures in B and insert `sorry` at broken proofs
62	8. Verify compilation across all affected files
63	9. Write your report to `task_results/refactor.md`
64	
65	## Handling Cascading Changes
66	
67	When you change a definition, expect downstream breakage. Handle it systematically:
68	
69	1. **Type mismatches:** Update signatures to match the new definition. Use the mathematical justification to determine the correct new types. This is your job.
70	2. **Broken proofs:** Insert `sorry`. This is the prover's job.
71	3. **Missing fields (if you changed a structure):** Add the new fields with `sorry` default values, or update construction sites.
72	4. **Import changes:** If you move or rename declarations, update imports in all affected files.
73	
74	## Logging
75	
76	Write your report to `task_results/refactor.md`. This report is the primary communication channel back to the plan agent — be precise and thorough.
77	
78	```markdown
79	# Refactor Report
80	
81	## Status
82	<COMPLETE or INCOMPLETE>
83	<If INCOMPLETE, explain exactly which changes could not be made and why.>
84	
85	## Directive
86	<Copy the Problem and Changes sections from the directive you received.>
87	
88	## Changes Made
89	
90	### File: <path>
91	- **What:** <description of change>
92	- **Why:** <from directive>
93	- **Cascading:** <list of files that broke and were fixed>
94	
95	### File: <path>
96	...
97	
98	## New Sorries Introduced
99	- `<file>:<line>` — <brief description of what proof broke and why>
100	- ...
101	
102	## Compilation Status
103	- <file>: compiles / errors (describe)
104	- ...
105	
106	## Notes for Plan Agent
107	<Anything the plan agent should know:
108	- Unexpected complications encountered
109	- Additional changes you think are needed but did NOT make (per the rules)
110	- Whether the mathematical justification was sufficient to guide cascading fixes
111	- Suggested follow-up refactors for the next iteration>
112	```
113	
114	The **Status** field is critical: if you write `INCOMPLETE`, the plan agent knows it may need to write another directive in the next iteration. If you write `COMPLETE`, the plan agent will proceed to assign provers to the new sorries.
115	
116	## Write Permissions
117	
118	| File | Permission |
119	|------|-----------|
120	| Any `.lean` file | **read + write** |
121	| `task_results/refactor.md` | **write** |
122	| All other state files | **read only** |

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafAb
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: sheafification, Ext, and the structure sheaf
10	
11	Phase A step 5 (per `STRATEGY.md`):
12	
13	* **Prerequisites** (iter-005, closed): the `HasSheafify` and `HasExt` instances
14	  on the topology of opens of an arbitrary topological space, valued in
15	  `ModuleCat k`. The `Linear k` enrichment on the resulting sheaf category is
16	  auto-inferable from Mathlib and therefore needs no scaffold here; together
17	  with `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.instModule` it gives the path to a
18	  `Module k` structure on `Ext` groups.
19	* **Main** (iter-006, scaffolded here): the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme
20	  `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))` viewed as a sheaf of `k`-modules. Built
21	  from the structure-morphism-derived ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` (helpers (1)–(5))
22	  and packaged as the presheaf `toModuleKPresheaf` (6), proven to be a sheaf
23	  by `toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (7), and bundled as `toModuleKSheaf` (8).
24	
25	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
26	
27	## Status (iteration 006 — refactor scaffold)
28	
29	The two prerequisite declarations (lines below) are honestly closed (iter-005).
30	The eight Phase A step 5 main declarations are scaffolded as `sorry`. The
31	iter-006 prover round is responsible for filling them.
32	-/
33	
34	set_option autoImplicit false
35	
36	universe u
37	
38	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
39	
40	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
41	
42	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (a): sheafification on the topology of opens of
43	any topological space, valued in `ModuleCat k`. Inferable from Mathlib's
44	small-site / concrete-category sheafification API; the prover's task is the
45	universe pinning, mirroring the iter-004 `instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
46	instance instHasSheafify_Opens_ModuleCatK
47	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
48	    CategoryTheory.HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
49	      (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
50	  inferInstance
51	
52	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (b): `Ext` on the sheaf category. The universe
53	annotation `HasExt.{u+1}` is forced by the morphism universe of
54	`Sheaf (Opens.gT X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`; mirrors the iter-004
55	`instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
56	noncomputable instance instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_ModuleCatK
57	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
58	    CategoryTheory.HasExt.{u+1}
59	      (CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
60	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
61	  CategoryTheory.HasExt.standard _
62	
63	end AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
64	
65	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
66	
67	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
68	
69	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (1): the ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` induced by the
70	structure morphism `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)` and the
71	restriction along `U ≤ ⊤`. -/
72	noncomputable def kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
73	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
74	    (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
75	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫
76	    C.hom.app ⊤ ≫ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
77	
78	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (2): `Γ(C, U)` is a `k`-algebra via the ring
79	map of `kToSection`. -/
80	noncomputable instance algebraSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
81	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
82	    Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
83	  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
84	
85	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (3): unfolding lemma for the algebra map. -/
86	lemma algebraMap_eq_kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
87	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
88	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
89	  rfl
90	
91	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
92	natural in `U`. -/
93	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
94	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
95	    kToSection C U ≫ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
96	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
97	  rfl
98	
99	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
100	`algebraMap`. -/
101	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
102	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
103	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
104	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
105	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
106	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ _) => g.hom r)
107	    (kToSection_naturality (C := C) f)
108	
109	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
110	
111	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
112	
113	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
114	
115	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (6): the presheaf of `k`-modules built from
116	`O_C` and the structure-morphism algebra structure. -/
117	noncomputable def toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
118	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
119	  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
120	  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
121	    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
122	      map_add' := fun x y => by
123	        simp [(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom.map_add]
124	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
125	        simp only [Algebra.smul_def, RingHom.map_mul, RingHom.id_apply]
126	        congr 1
127	        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
128	          (C := C) f r }
129	  map_id U := by
130	    ext x
131	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
132	      ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
133	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id U)) x
134	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
135	    ext x
136	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
137	      ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
138	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_comp f g)) x
139	
140	/-- Object-evaluation simp lemma for `toModuleKPresheaf`. Definitionally true
141	by construction; tagged `@[simp]` so consumers can rewrite without unfolding
142	the constructor. Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007). -/
143	@[simp] lemma toModuleKPresheaf_obj (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
144	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
145	    (toModuleKPresheaf C).obj U = ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
146	  rfl
147	
148	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (7): the presheaf of (6) is a sheaf for the
149	Grothendieck topology of opens of `C.left.toTopCat`. -/
150	lemma toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
151	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
152	      (toModuleKPresheaf C) := by
153	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _ (CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
154	  convert (Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _
155	      (CategoryTheory.forget CommRingCat.{u})).mp C.left.sheaf.property using 1
156	
157	/-- Phase A step 5 main: the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme as a sheaf
158	of `k`-modules. Bundles `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6) and
159	`toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (helper 7) into the standard `Sheaf` shape. -/
160	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
161	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
162	  ⟨toModuleKPresheaf C, toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf C⟩
163	
164	/-- Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007): forget-and-recover natural iso between
165	the iter-006 `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf and the iter-004
166	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf. The two sheaves agree on the nose at
167	the underlying-presheaf level: forgetting from `ModuleCat k` to `AddCommGrpCat`
168	via `forget₂` recovers the iter-004 `toAbSheaf C.left`. Closure body
169	`Iso.refl _`; probe-confirmed (`lean_run_code`, iter-007 plan-agent). -/
170	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare
171	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
172	    (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
173	        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)
174	      ≅ toAbSheaf C.left :=
175	  Iso.refl _
176	
177	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
178

1	# archon-protected.yaml
2	# Declarations whose signatures must not be modified by any agent.
3	# The mathematician owns these; agents are read-only on them.
4	
5	AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean:
6	  - AlgebraicGeometry.genus
7	
8	AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean:
9	  - AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian
10	  - AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian.instGrpObj
11	  - AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus
12	  - AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian.instIsProper
13	  - AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian.instGeometricallyIrreducible
14	
15	AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean:
16	  - AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian.ofCurve

input.code:
import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK

open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory Limits Opposite

universe v u

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

noncomputable abbrev HModuleProbe
    (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) : Type (u+1) :=
  CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext
    ((CategoryTheory.constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n

variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (X : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))

noncomputable example : ℕ := Module.finrank k (HModuleProbe k (toModuleKSheaf X) 1)

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare
    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
    (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)
      ≅ toAbSheaf C.left :=
  Iso.refl _

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
input.new_string:
noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare
    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
    (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)
      ≅ toAbSheaf C.left :=
  Iso.refl _

/-- Phase A step 5/6 bridge (iter-009 scaffold): the parallel `Sheaf.H` for
`ModuleCat k`-valued sheaves. Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H` is
parameterised over `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` only, so closing `genus` honestly
requires this `ModuleCat k`-flavoured version. The result carries `Module k`
automatically via `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.instModule`, and
`Module.finrank k` is therefore well-defined on it. The declaration is a
`noncomputable abbrev` (rather than `def`) so that instance synthesis sees
through the wrapper to find `Module k` and `AddCommGroup` instances. -/
noncomputable abbrev HModule
    (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) : Type (u+1) := sorry

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

# Refactor Report — iter-009

## Status

**COMPLETE**

The single-declaration scaffold landed cleanly. The probe-confirmed body (with
`sorry` body, per directive §3) typechecks; the universe-line change does not
disturb any prior declaration; all other files in `AlgebraicJacobian/` compile
with the same warning footprint as before this iteration.

## Directive

**Problem (paraphrased from §1):** Closing `protected genus` honestly as
`Module.finrank k (H¹(C, O_C))` requires a `Module k` structure on `H¹`.
Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H` is parameterised over the value category
`AddCommGrpCat` only, so it does not deliver an `Ext` in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat
k)` and therefore does not pick up `Abelian.Ext.instModule` automatically. The
fix is to mirror Mathlib's `Sheaf.H` construction with `AddCommGrpCat` replaced
by `ModuleCat k` and the unit object replaced by `ModuleCat.of k k`.

**Changes requested (§3):**

1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`:
   - Change `universe u` → `universe u v` (single line, with order `u v`).
   - Append a single new `noncomputable abbrev HModule …` declaration just
     before the closing `end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`, with body `sorry`
     (the prover round will close it with the probe-confirmed expression).
2. No other files touched. `archon-protected.yaml` not touched.

## Sanity-check probe (§2)

**Outcome:** PASS, no drift.

Ran the §2 probe verbatim via `lean_run_code`:

```
{"success":true,"diagnostics":[]}
```

The body
`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext ((CategoryTheory.constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n`
typechecks against the current Mathlib snapshot. The specialisation
`Module.finrank k (HModuleProbe k (toModuleKSheaf X) 1)` typechecks with no
warnings. No name drift; the directive's signature is drop-in. The scaffold
landed exactly the signature from §3.1.2, with `sorry` body per the directive's
phrasing (the body itself is queued for the iter-009 prover round).

## Changes Made

### File: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`

- **What (universe line):** L36 changed from `universe u` to `universe u v`.
  Preserves the existing `u` binding; the new `v` is consumed by `HModule`'s
  `{C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C]` parameters and is otherwise inert for the
  iter-005/006/007 declarations above.
- **What (new declaration):** Appended `noncomputable abbrev HModule` at
  L185–L189, immediately after `toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare` (L170–L175) and
  immediately before the existing `end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` (now L191).
  Doc-comment at L177–L184 follows the directive's phrasing. Body is `sorry`;
  the prover round fills it with the probe-confirmed
  `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext ((CategoryTheory.constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n`.
- **Why:** §1 (parallel `Sheaf.H` for `ModuleCat k`) — the `HModule` shape is
  what closes `genus` honestly, by virtue of `Abelian.Ext.instModule` giving a
  `Module k` structure that `Module.finrank k` then consumes.
- **Cascading:** none. The new declaration is additive; the universe-line
  change is invisible to all prior declarations (they continue to use `u`
  only).

### Diff summary (line numbers, post-refactor)

| Region | Pre | Post | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| Universe declaration | L36 `universe u` | L36 `universe u v` | append `v` |
| `toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare` close | L175 | L175 | unchanged |
| Doc-comment for `HModule` | — | L177–L184 | new |
| `HModule` declaration | — | L185–L189 | new (signature + `sorry` body) |
| `end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` | L177 | L191 | shifted +14 lines |

§4 (optional docstring update): skipped to keep the diff minimal.

### Other files

No other `.lean` file modified. `archon-protected.yaml` not touched.

## New Sorries Introduced

- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean:189` — `HModule`
  body. Per §3.1.2, the prover round will replace `sorry` with the
  probe-confirmed expression
  `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext ((CategoryTheory.constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n`.

## Compilation Status (§6)

Verbatim `lean_diagnostic_messages` output, post-refactor:

- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`:
  `{"result":{"success":true,"items":[{"severity":"warning","message":"declaration uses \`sorry\`","line":185,"column":22}],"failed_dependencies":[]}}`
  (the line:column pair points at the `abbrev HModule` opener — the only
  warning is the expected `declaration uses 'sorry'` on the new declaration).

Other touched/dependent files (zero new diagnostics; prior warnings unchanged):

- `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` — 3 sorry warnings (unchanged: lines
  34, 39, 49 — protected `Jacobian.ofCurve`, `comp_ofCurve`,
  `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` — 1 sorry warning (unchanged: line 87,
  protected `genus`).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/SheafCompose.lean` — `success: true, items: []`
  (unchanged).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafAb.lean` — `success: true,
  items: []` (unchanged).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` — 5 sorry warnings (unchanged: lines
  68, 85, 92, 100, 107 — protected `Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`,
  `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`,
  `instGeometricallyIrreducible`).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Rigidity.lean` — `success: true, items: []` (unchanged).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean` — `success: true, items: []`
  (unchanged).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` — 1 sorry warning (unchanged: line
  185, `PicardFunctor.representable`).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FunctorAb.lean` — `success: true, items: []`
  (unchanged).

Zero errors; zero unrelated warnings; zero failed dependencies anywhere.

## Sorry Count (§5)

Verified via `${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN} ${LEAN4_SCRIPTS}/sorry_analyzer.py
AlgebraicJacobian/`:

- **Pre-refactor:** 10 sorries.
- **Post-refactor:** 11 sorries (+1 = the new `HModule:189`).

Post-refactor sorry locations (in order):

1. `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean:35` — `def ofCurve` (protected)
2. `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean:41` — `lemma comp_ofCurve` (protected)
3. `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean:53` — `theorem exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (protected)
4. `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean:96` — `def genus` (protected)
5. `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean:77` — `def Jacobian` (protected)
6. `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean:88` — `instance instGrpObj` (protected)
7. `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean:97` — `instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus` (protected)
8. `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean:104` — `instance instIsProper` (protected)
9. `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean:114` — `instance instGeometricallyIrreducible` (protected)
10. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean:189`** — `abbrev HModule` (NEW; iter-009 prover target)
11. `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean:190` — `theorem PicardFunctor.representable`

The +1 is exactly the expected new `HModule` sorry at L189; all other sorries
are at the same locations as pre-refactor (modulo cosmetic numbering).

## Adjustments

None. The probe (§2) passed verbatim, so the directive's signature was
landed exactly as written.

## Notes for Plan Agent

- Probe outcome was clean; no Mathlib drift. The iter-009 prover round can
  use the probe-confirmed body
  `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext ((CategoryTheory.constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n`
  with no qualification adjustments.
- The §4 optional docstring update was skipped to keep the diff minimal
  (per §4: "if it complicates the diff, skip it"). If the plan agent wants
  it, that's a one-line addition in the file's leading docstring (currently
  L8–L32) and can be folded into the prover round or a future cosmetic pass.
- No additional refactors observed as needed for the next iteration. The
  iter-005/006/007 declarations above did not need to be touched, and the
  universe-line change is visibly inert for them (their bodies depend only
  on `u`).
- The `HModule` signature uses `[Field k]` rather than `[CommRing k]`
  (consistent with §3.1.2 reason 2). This is a deliberate divergence from
  the iter-005 prerequisites' `[CommRing k]` weakening — `Module.finrank`
  needs the field structure. If a future iteration needs an `HModule`
  variant over a commutative ring (e.g. for relative cohomology), it would
  be a separate declaration; this one is pinned to the field setting where
  `genus` lives.
